name: Pic de Cagire and Pic de l'Escalette Loop from Col de Menté
name_original: Le Pic de l’Escalette et le Pic Cagire – randonnée au départ du col de Menté
description: Hike a difficult 6.7-mile loop from Col de Menté to Pic de l'Escalette and Pic Cagire, gaining 2515 feet for panoramic Pyrénées views.
region_intro: Hiking around Boutx offers access to the Pyrenean terrain, characterized by mountainous and heavily forested environments. The commune features a significant altitude range, from 560 to 1,850 meters (1,840–6,070 ft), providing varied topography. Prominent features include the Col de Menté mountain pass and several peaks like Pic de Cagire and Pic de l'Escalette. The area is also rich in biodiversity, with extensive woodlands and mountain streams. Best hiking trails around Boutx The most popular hiking route is Pic de Cagire and Pic de l'Escalette Loop from Col de Menté, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the Pyrenees. Another top favourite among local hikers is Escalette Peak – Pic de l'Escalette (pass) loop from Col de Menté, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This trail navigates mountainous terrain with views of the pass. Local hikers also love the Bergerie du Col de Menté – Menté Pass loop from Col de Menté, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail leading through wooded areas near Col de Menté, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Hiking around Boutx is defined by Pyrenean peaks, dense woodlands, and mountain passes like Col de Menté. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging climbs. The routes in Boutx are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 390 reviews. More than 3,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Boutx's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking difficulty can I expect in Boutx?
A: Boutx offers a wide range of hiking difficulties, from easy strolls to challenging climbs. The area features varied topography with altitudes ranging from 560 to 1,850 meters (1,840–6,070 ft). You'll find 8 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and 10 difficult routes, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Boutx?
A: Yes, Boutx has several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26214026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bergerie du Col de Menté – Menté Pass loop from Col de Menté&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.2 miles (5.1 km) long and leads through wooded areas near Col de Menté. This trail is generally completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes.

Q: Can I find challenging, long-distance hikes in the Boutx area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Boutx offers demanding routes with significant elevation gain. A notable difficult trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958331011/pic-de-cagire-and-pic-de-l-escalette-loop-from-col-de-mente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de Cagire and Pic de l'Escalette Loop from Col de Menté&lt;/a&gt;. This 6.7-mile (10.8 km) route involves over 760 meters of ascent and offers panoramic Pyrenees views, typically taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes around Boutx?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Boutx are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5348660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escalette Peak – Pic de l'Escalette (pass) loop from Col de Menté&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.3-mile (10.1 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39240907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menté Pass – Pic de l'Escalette (pass) loop from Col de Menté&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 10 km.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the hiking trails in Boutx?
A: Boutx is rich in natural features and offers stunning viewpoints. You can hike to prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/466014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de Cagire&lt;/a&gt;, which provides breathtaking 360-degree views of the plain and higher Pyrenean peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menté Pass&lt;/a&gt; is another key access point offering panoramic vistas. The area also features beautiful mountain streams and dense forests.

Q: Are there any lakes or water features to explore near Boutx?
A: Yes, the region around Boutx is part of the Garonne basin and features numerous mountain streams. For larger water bodies, you can explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5990187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Saint-Pé-d'Ardet&lt;/a&gt; or the more distant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4199800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang d'Araing&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular natural monuments in the vicinity.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Boutx?
A: The hiking routes in Boutx are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 390 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse Pyrenean terrain, the stunning panoramic views from peaks like Pic de Cagire, and the immersive experience of walking through the heavily forested areas and rich biodiversity.

Q: Is Boutx a good destination for hiking through forests?
A: Boutx is characterized by its very wooded communal territory, making it an excellent destination for forest hikes. The commune boasts remarkable natural heritage, including two Natura 2000 sites. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20531613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menté Pass – Forest loop from Col de Menté&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate 11.6 km experience through these dense woodlands.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Boutx?
A: The Pyrenean terrain around Boutx is generally best for hiking from spring through autumn. During these seasons, the weather is typically more stable, and trails are clear of snow, allowing access to higher altitudes and offering pleasant conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes and rich biodiversity.

Q: Are there any other notable mountain passes to visit near Boutx?
A: Beyond the prominent Col de Menté, the region around Boutx features several other significant mountain passes. You can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portet-d'Aspet pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/853251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col des Ares&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1816303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buret Pass&lt;/a&gt;, all of which offer unique views and access to different parts of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any amenities or places to stay near the hiking trails in Boutx?
A: While Boutx is a small commune, the nearby Mourtis ski resort, accessible via the Col de Menté, provides some amenities. For accommodation and dining, you would typically find options in the surrounding villages and towns in the Haute-Garonne department, catering to visitors exploring the Pyrenees.
tips:
At the end of the forest road from the Col de Menté is a Sheepfold and a refuge.
The animals are free during the day and take shelter in the evening.
It is impressive to see the sheepdog leading the flock.
From the Col de Menté to Le Cagire, the Pic de l'Escalette is a very pleasant first stop. Magnificent view of the Pyrenees, the plain and the Cagire.
A few tens of meters really on the ridge line with a slightly delicate passage where you thank the grip of your shoes ...
A statue that few notice, concentrated on the path so as not to stumble. It is a regular at Cagire who made us discover it. You have to lift your head a little 😉 ...

I would like to make hikers aware that this route can become particularly dangerous in winter, particularly because of the ridge towards the Pas de l'Ane which can become covered in ice. In this case, ice axes and mountaineering crampons are essential. 2 people died at this location on 1/1/2025, which triggered access bans. I have taken steps to have these bans lifted while raising awareness in a constructive way. Take the time to find out about the snow conditions before undertaking this hike. Enjoy your walks!
Unforgettable viewpoint over the Pyrenees chain.

faqs.question: Where does the hike to Le Pic de l'Escalette and Pic Cagire start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The hike begins from the Col de Menté. There is parking available at the Col de Menté, which serves as the primary trailhead for this route.

faqs.question: How challenging is this hike?
faqs.answer: This hike is rated as difficult. The full loop, which includes Pic de Cagire, Pique Poque, Sommet des Parets, and Pic de l'Escalette, is considered 'Montagnard' (mountaineer) due to steep sections, exposed ridge lines, and parts requiring a good sense of orientation. Some passages may even require using your hands for stability. It's best avoided in strong winds or wet conditions due to dangerous polished rocks.

faqs.question: How long does this hike typically take?
faqs.answer: The full loop, covering approximately 10.8 km with about 767 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 5 hours to complete. An out-and-back route to Pic Cagire is slightly less demanding, taking about 4.5 hours.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain, starting with a steep climb through a pleasant forest. As you ascend, it transitions to open views, including walking along a ridge. You'll find well-marked paths, some off-trail sections, and more exposed, sometimes rocky, areas along the ridge lines. Be prepared for sections where you might need to use your hands for stability.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The hike offers magnificent panoramic views, especially from the summits. Key highlights include the Bergerie du Col de Menté, Pic de l'Escalette (pass), the scenic Ridge line, the Statue de la Vierge, and the impressive Pic de Cagire, known for its 360-degree views. You'll also pass a Viewpoint on the Rocher des Graues.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, but given the 'difficult' rating and sections requiring agile footing and potentially using hands, ensure your dog is accustomed to challenging mountain terrain. Always keep your dog under control, especially near livestock or other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is typically during dry conditions, from late spring to early autumn, when the weather is stable and the paths are clear of snow and ice. Avoid strong winds or wet conditions, as polished rocks on the ridge can become very dangerous.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail in the Pyrénées.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other trails. You may encounter parts of the Pedals d'Occitània, Voie Normale du Cagire, Tour du Cagire-Burat, and Passage Direct du Pas de l'Âne au Col de Caube.

faqs.question: Is there a less demanding alternative if I want to experience Pic Cagire?
faqs.answer: Yes, an out-and-back route to Pic Cagire, often via the Col du Pas de l'Âne, is considered less demanding. This option is rated 'Randonneur' (hiker) with about 850 meters of elevation gain over 12 km and takes around 4.5 hours, offering a slightly easier way to reach the summit.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or shelters along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail passes by the Cabane de Larreix, which is a well-maintained hut offering amenities like a fireplace, tables, benches, and fresh water. This can serve as a good resting point.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint on the Rocher des Graues
